at_rest.evidence: apps/web-platform/infra/workspaces-cutover.sh:2030,2041 (cryptsetup luksFormat/luksOpen, MAPPER_NAME=${WORKSPACES_MAPPER_NAME:-workspaces}); key: random_password.workspaces_luks + doppler_secret.workspaces_luks_key (workspaces-luks.tf); mount gate: soleur-host-bootstrap.sh:564-565
at_rest.defends_against: a seized/RMA'd or snapshot-imaged Hetzner block volume: contents are unreadable without the Doppler-held LUKS passphrase
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked service-role credential, an RLS bypass, an SSRF reaching the store, or any read on the live host where the mapper is already unlocked

at_rest.evidence: apps/web-platform/infra/cloud-init-git-data.yml:160,163 (cryptsetup luksFormat/luksOpen git-data); key: random_password.git_data_luks + doppler_secret.git_data_luks_key (git-data-luks.tf); fstab: cloud-init-git-data.yml:170
at_rest.defends_against: a seized/RMA'd or snapshot-imaged git-data block volume: unreadable without the Doppler-held passphrase
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked credential, an app-layer read on the unlocked host, or exfiltration via a compromised git-data process
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:no per-volume host posture probe on the git-data host yet; tracked #6897

at_rest.defends_against: nothing at the volume layer; superseded on web-1 by hcloud_volume.workspaces_luks (cutover certified 2026-07-23, verify run 30040444418)
at_rest.does_not_defend: a seized/snapshot disk exposes any workspace data still resident on this volume
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:host attachment state not pulled in the code-sourced audit; tracked #6897

exception.justification: superseded plaintext resource retained as the pre-cutover rollback backstop; web-1 /mnt/data now runs on the LUKS workspaces_luks mapper
exception.reevaluate_when: the workspaces_luks cutover is confirmed irreversible and the plaintext volume can be detached and destroyed

at_rest.defends_against: nothing at the volume layer; the pre-cutover rollback backstop pending the DL-2 wipe
at_rest.does_not_defend: a seized/snapshot disk exposes any git data still resident on this volume
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:host attachment state not pulled in the code-sourced audit; tracked #6897

exception.justification: plaintext rollback backstop for the git-data LUKS cutover, pending the DL-2 destructive wipe
exception.reevaluate_when: the git_data_luks cutover is confirmed and the DL-2 wipe of the plaintext volume runs

at_rest.does_not_defend: a seized/snapshot disk exposes the Inngest queue + run-state AOF, i.e. in-flight job payloads (user prompts and agent output)
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:the Hetzner API is blind to guest-side LUKS and no inngest-host posture probe exists; tracked #6894

exception.justification: highest-sensitivity plaintext store; encryption is a guest-side LUKS cutover with its own drain/freeze/blast-radius analysis, out of scope for the audit PR

at_rest.defends_against: nothing at the volume layer; integrity of the mirror comes from cosign digest-pinning, not at-rest encryption
at_rest.does_not_defend: a seized/snapshot disk exposes OCI blobs + cosign signatures (a disposable GHCR mirror; low sensitivity)

exception.justification: disposable GHCR mirror; low sensitivity; destroy+recreate as a LUKS volume is cheap but not urgent
exception.reevaluate_when: the registry volume is recreated with guest-side LUKS

at_rest.evidence: Cloudflare R2 stores objects encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM (developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/data-security/); Cloudflare holds an AICPA SOC 2 Type II attestation (Security/Confidentiality/Availability) with R2 in scope per the Cloudflare Trust Hub — the report is available under NDA, the attestation's existence and scope are public; holds CLA (Contributor License Agreement) signing evidence records.
at_rest.defends_against: Cloudflare-managed at-rest encryption of stored objects against physical-media compromise in Cloudflare's infrastructure
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked R2 API token, an application-layer read, or any access via the credentials that legitimately reach the bucket; a provider-managed key is not a customer-held key
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:provider-managed at-rest; SOC 2 Type II report is NDA-gated — public attestation cited via mechanism/attestation_url; no customer-side live probe of Cloudflare disk encryption

at_rest.evidence: Cloudflare R2 stores objects encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM (developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/data-security/); Cloudflare holds an AICPA SOC 2 Type II attestation (Security/Confidentiality/Availability) with R2 in scope per the Cloudflare Trust Hub — the report is available under NDA, the attestation's existence and scope are public; holds LUKS-header escrow, itself useless without the separately-held passphrase
at_rest.defends_against: physical-media compromise of the escrowed LUKS headers in Cloudflare's infrastructure
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked R2 token; note a LUKS header alone cannot decrypt a volume without the passphrase held in a separate Doppler config
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:provider-managed at-rest; SOC 2 Type II report is NDA-gated — public attestation cited via mechanism/attestation_url; no customer-side live probe of Cloudflare disk encryption

at_rest.evidence: Supabase encrypts Postgres data at rest with AES-256 (Supabase security documentation)
at_rest.defends_against: physical-media compromise of the managed Postgres storage
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked service-role key, an RLS bypass, or any query via a legitimate connection; the encryption key is provider-managed
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:named SOC 2 attestation formalization pending; tracked #6911

at_rest.evidence: Supabase Postgres AES-256 at-rest encryption (Supabase security documentation); the Inngest metadata Postgres project
at_rest.defends_against: physical-media compromise of the managed Postgres storage
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked credential or an application-layer read
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:named SOC 2 attestation formalization pending; tracked #6911

at_rest.evidence: Doppler encrypts secrets at rest with AES-256-GCM (Doppler security documentation)
at_rest.defends_against: physical-media compromise of Doppler's secret storage
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked Doppler service token or a compromised host with a live token; the LUKS passphrases themselves live here, so a Doppler compromise is a key-custody compromise
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:named SOC 2 attestation formalization pending; tracked #6911

at_rest.evidence: Better Stack Logs encrypts stored log data at rest (Better Stack security documentation); source 2457081
at_rest.defends_against: physical-media compromise of the log store
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked Better Stack API token; note log sanitization at the emitter is the primary control against sensitive data reaching the sink
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:named SOC 2 attestation formalization pending; tracked #6911

at_rest.evidence: Cloudflare R2 stores objects encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM (developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/data-security/); Cloudflare holds an AICPA SOC 2 Type II attestation (Security/Confidentiality/Availability) with R2 in scope per the Cloudflare Trust Hub — the report is available under NDA, the attestation's existence and scope are public; the R2-backed Terraform state bucket (soleur-terraform-state, main.tf:2-6) — in-repo comments in doppler-write-token.tf:23 / ghcr-minter-doppler-token.tf:36 previously asserted 'encrypted' with no attestation, now substantiated
at_rest.defends_against: physical-media compromise of the Terraform state store
at_rest.does_not_defend: a leaked R2 token; Terraform state contains resource metadata and any non-sensitive-marked values
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:provider-managed at-rest; SOC 2 Type II report is NDA-gated — public attestation cited via mechanism/attestation_url; no customer-side live probe of Cloudflare disk encryption

at_rest.evidence: model.c4:218 models sessionStore as 'TLS, requirepass, EU (Phase 4a)'; at-rest posture unstated in code
at_rest.defends_against: nothing asserted at the at-rest layer pending confirmation
at_rest.does_not_defend: a seized/snapshot disk may expose session state until the at-rest posture is confirmed or LUKS is applied
at_rest.live_verification: unavailable:session-store host posture not pulled in the code-sourced audit; tracked #6897

exception.justification: at-rest posture unconfirmed from code alone; recorded as an exception rather than asserted, pending a live pull
exception.reevaluate_when: the session-store host at-rest posture is measured (or LUKS applied)

in_transit.does_not_defend: a leaked anon/service key; TLS protects the channel, not the credential

in_transit.tls: none (plain HTTP on the private network by design)
in_transit.does_not_defend: a passive on-net attacker could read pulled image bytes; integrity (not confidentiality) is provided by cosign digest-pinning

exception.justification: private-network-only registry link; integrity comes from cosign digest-pinning, not TLS; image bytes are public OCI layers
exception.reevaluate_when: the registry is exposed beyond the private network, or TLS is added to the link
